Thought 2: The murder occurred in the labyrinth, and Charlotte had exclusive access to it during the time of the murder. She was alone there for hours, which gives her the opportunity. She also has a strong motive (protecting her sister) and access to the murder weapon (corkscrew). Rachel was seen with Samuel entering her apartment, but the murder didn't happen there—it happened in the labyrinth. While Rachel has a violent past and Samuel's belongings were in her trash, this might be a red herring or unrelated (perhaps she was involved with him but didn't kill him in the labyrinth). The key is the location of the murder: the labyrinth, which Charlotte controlled.
Action 2: Do[Focus on who had access to the labyrinth at the time of the murder and the significance of the corkscrew]
